implementationStatus: No lang/pages/**/sw.json pilot was created because the support is mechanically present but not route-safe for current Swahili source ownership.
selectedPilotFamily: Country PAYE metadata/source-pack pilot after route-alias support exists
whyThisFamily: PAYE has English source pages, explicit Swahili route mappings in SW_SLUG_TO_EN, active post-processing owner scripts/fix-sw-paye-custom-ui.js, and 54 already-exposed country pages. It is safer than VAT/TIN because VAT lacks a tracked generator or matching English country source pages.

findings.evidence: build-i18n.js loads lang/pages/<pagePath>/<lang>.json and supports page.title, page.metaDescription, page.ogTitle, page.ogDescription and page.h1 overrides plus <lang>.body.html for article-body pages.
buildOutputPath(pagePath, sw) writes to /sw/<English pagePath>/; getSwahiliUrl only affects hreflang URL generation, not where generated HTML is written.
SW_SLUG_TO_EN maps major hubs, PAYE pages and agriculture pages, but not VAT/TIN/HR country subpages.
Prompt 71 ledger found 54 VAT pages already exposed but no tracked generator; local inspection did not find matching English /<country>/kikokotoo-vat/ source pages.
English sources like ghana/gh-paye.html exist, Swahili routes like /sw/ghana/kikokotoo-kodi-mshahara/ exist, and fix-sw-paye-custom-ui.js owns cleanup.

findings.status: unsafe for pilot without overwrite policy
findings.evidence: A pack like lang/pages/api/sw.json would make future build-i18n --all treat sw/api/index.html as generated output and overwrite the curated API bridge.

recommendedPilotSteps: Add a route-alias aware output resolver for Swahili page packs, using SW_SLUG_TO_EN in reverse or an explicit SW_EN_TO_ROUTE map.
Add a dry-run mode that reports the exact output file for a given page pack before writing.
Create one metadata-only pack for ghana/gh-paye -> /sw/ghana/kikokotoo-kodi-mshahara/ after the resolver can target the existing canonical Swahili route.
Run node scripts/build-i18n.js --lang sw --page ghana/gh-paye --dry-run, then a targeted build only if the output path is the canonical Swahili route and diff is metadata-only.
Follow with npm run build:i18n:validate, npm run validate:hreflang, and targeted link check for /sw/ghana/kikokotoo-kodi-mshahara/.
blockedActions: Did not create lang/pages/ghana/gh-paye/sw.json because current output would not target the canonical Swahili route.
Did not create lang/pages/api/sw.json because it would make a future full i18n build overwrite a curated hand-authored bridge.
Did not attempt VAT/TIN page packs because no source generator or English source family is confirmed.
validation: Design-only report. No source HTML or build-i18n script changed, so no build was required by the prompt.
